text.title: Cloud Run Traffic Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the cloud-run-traffic-management methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 100% of users hit a regression at once with no rollback signal.
antipattern.root-cause: New revision deployed straight to 100% traffic without `--no-traffic`/canary.
antipattern.fix: Adopt canary: `--no-traffic --tag canary` → split 5% → 25% → 100% gated on monitoring.

antipattern.symptom: Rollback takes minutes and may pick up unrelated changes.
antipattern.root-cause: Rollback is performed by `git revert` + redeploy, not by revision pin.
antipattern.fix: Roll back via `gcloud run services update-traffic --to-revisions=<prev>=100`.

antipattern.symptom: Cloud Console crowded with orphan tags; on-call confusion during incidents.
antipattern.root-cause: `gcloud run revisions list --filter='tag:*'` shows tags older than the last promotion.
antipattern.fix: Add post-promote step that removes promotion tag; periodically prune.

antipattern.symptom: Experiments produce non-stationary signal because the tagged target keeps changing.
antipattern.root-cause: Traffic split targets `--to-tags`, with tag re-assigned during deploy.
antipattern.fix: Pin experiments to two stable revisions; never use tag as A/B target.
